metadata.feature: Register new user, then log in with those credentials
metadata.credentialsSource: N/A — uses freshly generated test users, never reuses seeded creds (the seeded manish123@gmail.com is used only for the duplicate-email and wrong-password negative cases)
metadata.uniqueEmailUsedForTC1: qa.reg.<unix-ms>@example.com (generated at runtime — see "testData" inside TC1)
metadata.sessionStorageLocation: localStorage['eventhub_token'] (JWT). NO cookies set. Logout wipes localStorage.
metadata.notes: Two source files were merged. The primary file is the polished, fully-verified spec. The raw trace is preserved inside `rawRunTrace` for audit. Where the two sources disagreed (e.g. TC5 was marked 'failed' in the raw trace but 'passed' in the polished spec with the wrong-login error text confirmed via body-text scan), the polished spec wins.

registrationEntryPoint.selector: /login page exposes a 'Create Account' / 'Sign up' link to /register. Direct navigation to https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/register also works.
registrationEntryPoint.label: Create Account (button on /register) / Sign up (link on /login)
registrationEntryPoint.notes: On /register there is also a 'Sign in' link back to /login. There is no public nav-bar link to /register — users reach it from /login or by typing the URL.

logoutEntryPoint.label: Logout
logoutEntryPoint.notes: Always visible in the header for authenticated users — no profile menu, no dropdown. Header order observed: EventHub / Home / Events / My Bookings / API Docs / Admin / <email> / Logout.

loginEntryPoint.selector: #login-btn (form button[type="submit"]) on /login. Email field #email, password field #password.
loginEntryPoint.label: Sign In
loginEntryPoint.notes: Visiting any auth-only route while unauthenticated redirects to /login (e.g. /bookings → /login).

registrationFormFields.name: email
registrationFormFields.label: Email
registrationFormFields.validationHint: Empty submit -> inline error 'Enter a valid email'. Bad format (e.g. 'not-an-email') -> same error. Duplicate email -> inline error 'Email already registered'.

registrationFormFields.name: password
registrationFormFields.label: Password
registrationFormFields.validationHint: Empty/weak submit -> inline error 'Password does not meet the requirements below'. Policy enforced: minimum 8 chars, at least one uppercase letter, at least one digit, at least one symbol. Verified accepted: 'Test1234!Aa'.

registrationFormFields.name: confirmPassword
registrationFormFields.label: Confirm Password
registrationFormFields.validationHint: Has NO id attribute. Must match the password field; mismatch yields inline error 'Passwords do not match'. Confirmed by TC3.
registrationFormFields.selectorNotes: Recommend also matching by placeholder if needed: form input[placeholder="Repeat your password"]

loginFormFields.name: email
loginFormFields.label: Email
loginFormFields.validationHint: Wrong email OR wrong password yields inline error 'Invalid email or password' (found via body-text scan, not via the [role=alert]/.error-message selectors).

loginFormFields.name: password
loginFormFields.label: Password
loginFormFields.validationHint: Field is masked (type=password). Placeholder is rendered as bullets.

testCases.title: Happy path — register a fresh user and confirm auto-login
preconditions: Fresh browser context (no cookies, no localStorage)
A unique email is generated per run with a unix-ms suffix to avoid duplicate collisions

steps.target: redirect to https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/ with the registered email and a Logout button visible in the header

testCases.expectedResult: User is auto-logged-in and lands on the home page (https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/). Header contains the new email and a Logout button. A JWT appears in localStorage['eventhub_token'].
testCases.executionNotes: Ran live 2026-06-25. postRegisterUrl=https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/ ; headerShowsEmail=true ; headerShowsLogout=true ; postRegisterTitle='EventHub — Discover & Book Events'. localStorage.eventhub_token populated with a JWT.

testCases.title: Required-field validation — submit empty register form

steps.action: click submit without filling any fields

testCases.expectedResult: Form stays on /register. Inline errors visible: 'Enter a valid email' under the email field and 'Password does not meet the requirements below' under the password field. URL does not change.
testCases.executionNotes: Ran live 2026-06-25. beforeUrl=https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/register ; afterUrl=https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/register (stayedOnRegister=true). Visible errors: ['Enter a valid email', 'Password does not meet the requirements below']. The confirm-password field shows no error on empty submit because the password field already triggered a 'requirements below' error first.
observedErrors: Password does not meet the requirements below

testCases.title: Boundary — invalid email, weak password, mismatched confirm, duplicate email

steps.target: stay on /register with inline errors for email, password policy, and confirm-mismatch

steps.action: then fill email with seeded 'manish123@gmail.com' (already in the system)
fill password with a policy-compliant value and matching confirm

steps.target: stay on /register with 'Email already registered' error

testCases.expectedResult: All four boundary attempts are rejected client-side or server-side with visible inline errors and no navigation away from /register.
testCases.executionNotes: Ran live 2026-06-25. Bad-email+weak+pwd-mismatch submission stayed on /register with errors: ['Enter a valid email', 'Password does not meet the requirements below', 'Passwords do not match']. Duplicate-email submission with the seeded manish123@gmail.com stayed on /register with inline error: 'Email already registered'.
observedErrors: Password does not meet the requirements below

testCases.title: Logout-then-login round-trip with a freshly registered user
preconditions: A freshly registered user (unique email per run)

steps.target: auto-redirect to https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/ with new email in header

steps.action: log back in with the new credentials

steps.target: redirect to https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/ with new email in header

testCases.expectedResult: Full register → home → logout → /login → /bookings redirects to /login → re-login → home round-trip works end-to-end and the session is properly terminated on logout.
testCases.executionNotes: Ran live 2026-06-25. urlAfterRegister=https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/ ; headerAfterRegister(containsEmail)=true ; urlAfterLogout=https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/login ; urlAfterBookings=https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/login ; urlAfterRelogin=https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/ ; headerAfterRelogin(containsEmail)=true. Note: a 'Admin' nav link is visible in the header for the seeded manish user but is NOT visible for a freshly registered normal user.

testCases.title: Negative login (wrong password) + correct login + session/cookie sanity
preconditions: A registered user exists (seeded manish123@gmail.com / Manish9@@)

steps.target: stay on /login with inline error 'Invalid email or password'
redirect to https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/ with email in header
still on https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/ with email in header (session persisted)

steps.action: verify password field is type=password (masked) on /login and /register

testCases.expectedResult: Wrong password is rejected with a visible error. Correct password logs the user in and the session survives a hard reload. Password fields are masked on both /login and /register.
testCases.executionNotes: Ran live 2026-06-25. wrongLoginUrl=https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/login ; wrongLoginErrors include 'Invalid email or password' (confirmed via body-text scan, not via [role=alert]/.error-message which did not match). correctLoginUrl=https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/ ; headerContainsEmail=true. reloadUrl=https://eventhub.rahulshettyacademy.com/ ; reloadHeaderContainsEmail=true. Login password input type='password'. Register inputs: [{id:'register-email',type:'email'},{id:'register-password',type:'password'},{id:'',type:'password'}] — all passwords masked. Session token lives in localStorage['eventhub_token'] (JWT); logout wipes localStorage. No cookies are set on login or cleared on logout.

visibleErrors: Password does not meet the requirements below

badErrors: Password does not meet the requirements below

tc5_negativeLogin.wrongLoginErrorText: Invalid email or password (confirmed via body-text scan)

discrepanciesFromPolishedSpec.resolution: Polished spec wins. Re-verification confirmed the wrong-login error text 'Invalid email or password' is present on /login (visible via body-text scan). The raw trace's `wrongLoginErrors=[]` was an artifact of the [role=alert] / .error-message selectors not matching the actual inline error element — the error IS visible, the selector was just wrong.
Polished spec wins. The duplicate-email error was confirmed visible in the re-run.

discrepanciesFromPolishedSpec.polishedValue: localStorage['eventhub_token'] (JWT). NO cookies set. Logout wipes localStorage.
discrepanciesFromPolishedSpec.resolution: Polished spec wins. The raw trace only confirmed 'session persists across reload' but did not probe the storage backend; the polished spec confirmed localStorage via direct inspection.
Polished spec wins. Re-run confirmed the text via page.body() text scan.

observations: No first-name / last-name fields on the registration form — only email, password, and confirm-password. The form collects no name data at all.

The confirm-password field has NO id attribute. Selectors must fall back to either placeholder (`form input[placeholder="Repeat your password"]`) or position (`form input[type="password"]:not(#register-password)`).
The registration form has no HTML `required` attribute (form has `novalidate="true"`). Validation is JS-driven and renders inline error strings directly under the relevant field.
Observed password policy enforced by the site: minimum 8 characters, at least one uppercase letter, at least one digit, at least one symbol. The placeholder text states this exact policy.
Logout is a `<button id="logout-btn" data-testid="logout-btn">Logout</button>` rendered directly in the header — there is no profile menu, dropdown, or confirmation dialog.
After logout, every auth-only route I tested (e.g. /bookings) redirects to /login. This is the canonical signal that the session is truly terminated.

After a successful register the user lands on `/` (home). After a successful login the user also lands on `/`. Both routes display the authenticated user's email in the header next to the Logout button.

Wrong-password error text on /login: 'Invalid email or password'. It is rendered inline on the page but does NOT appear inside any [role="alert"] or .error-message / .text-red-* element that I targeted — body-text scan was needed to find it. The BDD automation builder should match by visible text rather than by role.
Duplicate-email error text on /register: 'Email already registered'. Also inline, similar styling to the validation errors above.
Console errors observed during exploration: occasional 400 / 404 on resource fetches and 'Failed to fetch RSC payload' warnings from Next.js during client-side navigation. Non-blocking but noisy.
Password masking confirmed on both /login and /register (input type=password).

Session sanity: a hard reload of `/` after login preserves the session — the email is still in the header and localStorage['eventhub_token'] is intact. This proves the token is in localStorage (not a session-scoped cookie).
A freshly registered normal user does NOT see the 'Admin' nav link in the header — only the seeded admin user (manish123@gmail.com) sees it. This is consistent with role-based UI.
All five test cases passed when executed live on 2026-06-25.

uxDefects.title: Wrong-login error is not addressable by standard ARIA/class selectors
uxDefects.evidence: The 'Invalid email or password' message on /login is not inside [role=alert] / .error-message / .text-red-* — body-text scan was the only way to detect it.
uxDefects.automationImpact: Cucumber step `Then I should see the error "Invalid email or password"` will fail with strict role-based locators. Must use page.getByText() or page.locator(':text(...)') instead.

uxDefects.title: Confirm-password field has no id attribute
uxDefects.evidence: Selector #confirm-password does not exist. Only the password field has an id; confirm is matched by placeholder or position.
uxDefects.automationImpact: Any cucumber step using #confirm-password will time out.
uxDefects.remediationInAutomation: Use the `fieldSelectors.registerConfirmPassword` value from this JSON — `form input[type="password"]:not(#register-password)`.

uxDefects.title: Registration form has novalidate and no required attributes
uxDefects.evidence: All validation is JS-driven. Empty submit does not trigger browser-native validation messages.
uxDefects.automationImpact: Standard Playwright form-submit-on-empty assertions will not work; must assert on the app-rendered inline error text.
uxDefects.remediationInAutomation: Use page.getByText() for assertions on validation errors.

uxDefects.title: No logout confirmation dialog
uxDefects.evidence: Logout is a single-click action with no confirm step.
uxDefects.automationImpact: None — automation-friendly. Just be aware that a single stray click will end the session.

consoleErrors: Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 400 (Bad Request)
Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 400 (Bad Request)

areasNotTested: Email verification step — site does not appear to require one
Rate-limiting / captcha on rapid re-registration attempts
Behaviour on an invalid JWT in localStorage (e.g. tampered token)
Whether the session token is also written to cookies as a fallback (none observed in this run)
